description: Trigger discrimination for one loop's semantics and stopping (this skill) vs the budget it consumes (ai-cost-guardrail-designer), broken-state recovery (agent-failure-recovery), the in-batch harness seam (agent-harness-architect), multi-agent containment review (agent-containment-reviewer), and the DESIGN-vs-REVIEW seam against agent-goal-hijack-defender / ai-threat-modeler.

cases.prompt: Decide whether this task even needs an agentic loop, and if so design it: a hard iteration cap, policy rejections never retried, transient failures retried once on the identical input to tell flake from deterministic, and an empty result delivered honestly as empty.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — the single-shot decision, typed retryability, and honest terminal states are loop SEMANTICS. No budget authoring, no fleet containment, no attack review is requested.
cases.reason: The subject is one loop's shape, bounds, and stopping. The caps it clamps to are consumed from the cost skill, not authored here; nothing asks whether an attacker can manipulate the loop.

cases.prompt: Put loop and recursion cost bounds on our agents: per-run token budgets, tenant-level spend caps, cost-aware rate limits, and a kill switch when the burn rate spikes.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (pinned) — authoring the COST policy (budgets, caps, kill switch) vs designing the loop that consumes it. This prompt is entirely spend-side.
cases.reason: Cost bounds as policy are ai-cost-guardrail-designer. agentic-loop-designer consumes those caps as an input and owns what the loop DOES at the ceiling — the semantics, not the price.

cases.prompt: An agent run died mid-rebase and left the tree conflicted with a half-applied commit — get the working state back without losing work.
cases.reason: Recovering a broken working state is agent-failure-recovery (manual-only). agentic-loop-designer designs the loop structure — checkpoints, typed failures, resumable state — that such recovery plugs into; it does not perform recovery.

cases.prompt: Design the environment our agent runs in: one server-side mediation point, identity from credentials, a deny-by-default pre-flight ladder before the model runs, a closed tool registry, and fail-closed audit.
cases.note: IN-BATCH SEAM (pinned both ways) — the environment's gating is the harness; the loop runs INSIDE it and every iteration's calls still pass its ladder. Nothing here asks about iterations, retries, or stopping.
cases.reason: Mediation, ladder, registry, and audit are agent-harness-architect. agentic-loop-designer owns the loop's shape and bounds within that environment.

cases.prompt: Review whether our multi-agent pipeline contains failure: blast-radius isolation between agents, circuit breakers on inter-agent calls, drift detection against a baseline, and kill switches that actually sever authority.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — retry-storm and fan-out limits sound loop-adjacent, but this is the containment REVIEW of a multi-agent system, not the design of one loop's internal semantics.
cases.reason: Cross-agent cascade isolation, drift baselines, and kill switches are agent-containment-reviewer's review lane. agentic-loop-designer designs a single loop's iterations, retries, and stops.

cases.prompt: Review our agent's multi-step runs for manipulation: can a poisoned tool output or injected document substitute the objective or expand the scope mid-loop?
cases.note: DESIGN-vs-REVIEW SEAM (mandatory) — this is the attack review of loop/goal manipulation: agent-goal-hijack-defender owns the goal/plan layer, ai-threat-modeler the broader adversarial map. agentic-loop-designer PRODUCES the loop those skills review; it never runs the manipulation audit.
cases.reason: Attack review of loop manipulation belongs to the security cluster. This skill designs the loop's honest shape and stopping in the first place.
